2018}} |retired = yes }} '''James Darryl Peacock''' [[Order of the British Empire|MBE]], (born 14 December 1977) is an English motivational speaker, leadership mentor and former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. Peacock began his career with [[Bradford Bulls]] in 1998, which also included a loan spell at [[Featherstone Rovers]], he made a name for himself playing in the {{rlp|sr}} winning three [[Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]]s before a move to [[Leeds Rhinos]] in 2006. At Leeds, Peacock won six more [[Super League Grand Final|Grand Finals]] and became one of the most decorated players in British rugby league history. After briefly returning in 2015 Peacock played four games for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2016. Peacock made his [[England national rugby league team|England]] debut at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]] and his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] debut the following year as well as representing [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]]. ==Background== Born in [[Leeds]], Peacock is a product of [[Bradford Bulls]]' junior programme, having played as a junior for Stanningley RLFC. On his way to becoming a first team regular for Bradford, Jamie spent a month on loan at [[Featherstone Rovers]] making four appearances and scoring one try. He also enjoyed a spell at [[University of Wollongong]] in Australia as he learned his rugby trade.{{cite news|work=[[BBC Sport]] |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/7347248.stm |title=Peacock dares to dream |date=25 April 2008 |access-date=25 April 2008 |first=Paul |last=Fletcher |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/7347248.stm |archive-date=21 July 2010 |url-status=live }} ==Club career== ===Bradford=== Peacock made his Bradford Super League début in 1999. He made two starts and 16 substitute appearances for the club, scoring six tries that year. The following year he cemented his place in the side, helping Bradford to win [[2000 Challenge Cup]] success against Leeds at [[Murrayfield Stadium|Murrayfield]]. Peacock played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] as a {{rlp|sr}} in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]]. As [[Super League VI]] champions, the Bulls played against [[2001 NRL season|2001 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[2002 World Club Challenge]]. Peacock played as a {{rlp|sr}} in Bradford's victory. He made 30 appearances in 2002 as Bradford again returned to [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]] for the [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] only to lose out to a [[Sean Long]] drop goal. Peacock played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] as a {{rlp|sr}} in their [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]]. Having won [[Super League VIII]], Bradford played against [[2003 NRL season|2003 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]]. Peacock captained the Bulls as a {{rlp|sr}} in their 22–4 victory. Bradford reached the [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] losing out to Leeds. Peacock was honoured with the captaincy at Bradford Bulls in his final year at Odsal and played for the Bulls as a {{rlp|pr}} in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] victory against [[Leeds Rhinos]] at Old Trafford. During his time with Bradford he won every club honour available, in addition to winning the [[Man of Steel Award|Man of Steel]], Players' Player of the Year and Rugby League Writers' Player of the Year.{{cite news |title=Super League: Leeds' Jamie Peacock named Rugby League Writers' player of the year |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9491080/super-league-leeds-jamie-peacock-named-rugby-league-writers-player-of-the-year |access-date=31 August 2022 |work=Sky Sports |date=27 September 2014}} ===Leeds=== [[File:Jamie Peacock 1.jpg|thumb|right|Peacock playing for Leeds in 2015]] Peacock joined Leeds in 2006 from local rivals Bradford. He made his Leeds début against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] on the opening day of the season. He played in the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].{{cite news |title=2008 Grand Final |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm |work=BBC Sport |date=4 October 2008 |access-date=5 October 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm |archive-date=21 July 2010 |url-status=live }} and was also part of the 2007, 2009, 2011 and 2012 [[Super League Grand Final]] squads and team that won the [[World Club Challenge]] against [[Manly Sea Eagles]]. Peacock played a starring role in Leeds' [[2007 Super League Grand Final|33-6 Grand Final]] victory over then reigning champions' St Helens. He played a full 80 minutes to win his 4th Super League champions ring. He was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for both the 2008's [[Super League XIII]] and 2009's [[Super League XIV]] season.{{cite web |date=15 September 2008 |access-date=26 September 2008 |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755 |title=2008 engage Super League Dream Team |publisher=Super League |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755 |archive-date=21 July 2010 |url-status=dead }} He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}} He enjoyed a resurgence the following season, and was once again named in the Super League Dream Team in 2013, 2014, and 2015. Peacock played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|work=The web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/29/leeds-emphatically-shut-out-hull-kr-to-win-challenge-cup|title=Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup|work=The Guardian|date=29 August 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}{{cite news |title=Scoreboard |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |issue=2982 |date=31 August 2015 |page=31}}{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34086760 |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 0-50 Leeds Rhinos |work=BBC Sport |date=29 August 2015 |access-date=29 August 2015}} Peacock announced that he would retire from the sport upon the conclusion of the 2015 season{{cite web|url=http://www.totalrl.com/jamie-peacock-retire-end-2015|title=Jamie Peacock to retire at end of 2015|last1=Whitley|first1=Joe|date=27 January 2014|website=totalrl.com|publisher=Total Rugby League|access-date=23 June 2015}} joining [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] as a coach.{{cite news|last1=Press Association|title=Jamie Peacock joins Hull KR's coaching staff for his next challenge|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/mar/27/jamie-peacock-joins-hull-kr-coaching-staff|website=The Guardian|date=27 March 2015 |access-date=10 September 2015}} He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=The Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=7 November 2019}} ===Hull Kingston Rovers=== Peacock joined Hull KR as a coach for the 2016 Super League season. At the end of the regular season Hull KR finished 11th thus played in the [[2016 Super 8s#The Qualifiers|2016 Qualifiers]]. Towards the conclusion of the season, Peacock came out of retirement taking up the role of a player-coach due to squad injuries.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37288758|title=Jamie Peacock: Ex-England captain out of retirement to play for Hull KR|work=BBC Sport|date=6 September 2016|access-date=6 September 2016}} ==International career== [[File:Jamie Peacock.jpg|thumb|150px|left|Peacock representing England in 2008.]] Peacock earned himself a place in [[John Kear]]'s England side for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]]. He made four appearances in the World Cup that year, scoring an impressive six tries which including a hat trick against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]]. Peacock played for England in 2001 against Wales. Also in 2001, he capped the season by making his Great Britain début against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] scoring a try after just 86 seconds of his début in the first test victory at [[John Smith's Stadium|Huddersfield]]. He then become a permanent presence in the Great Britain squad making 14 appearances, all but three of which have been in the starting line up. Peacock won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for Great Britain while at Bradford Bulls in 2001 against Australia (2 matches), and Australia (sub), in 2002 against Australia, New Zealand, and New Zealand (sub) (2 matches), in 2003 against Australia (3 matches), in 2004 against Australia (3 matches), and New Zealand, in 2005 against Australia (2 matches), and New Zealand (2 matches). Peacock was then selected in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain team]] to compete in the end of season [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] tournament. In the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] he played as a {{rlp|sr}} in the Lions' 44–4 loss. Peacock played for Great Britain while at Leeds in 2006 against New Zealand (3 matches), and Australia (2 matches), in 2007 against New Zealand (3 matches). He played for England in 2008 against France. On 16 June 2008, Peacock was announced as the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England]] captain for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] in Australia.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm |title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up |work=BBC Sport |date=7 October 2008 |access-date=7 October 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm |archive-date=21 July 2010 |url-status=live }} In [[2008 Rugby League World Cup Group A|Group A]]'s first match against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] he played as a {{rlp|pr}} in England's victory. He played against Papua New Guinea, Australia, and New Zealand (2 matches), in 2009 against France (2 matches), New Zealand and Australia. He was selected to play for England against France in the one-off test in 2010.{{cite news |last=Hadfield |first=Dave |title=Widdop passes his first Test at a canter |work=[[The Independent]] |place=London, England |publisher=Independent Print Limited |date=13 June 2010 |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/widdop-passes-his-first-test-at--a-canter-1999087.html |url-status=live |access-date=17 February 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/widdop-passes-his-first-test-at--a-canter-1999087.html |archive-date=21 July 2010 }} On 26 June 2012, Peacock announced his retirement from international rugby league.{{cite web |newspaper=[[The Daily Telegraph]] |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/9355685/England-captain-Jamie-Peacock-announces-retirement-from-international-game.html |title=Jamie Peacock |access-date=26 June 2012 Britain Total||26||4 |- !colspan=2|Career Total||48||12 |} ==Honours== ===Club=== ;Bradford *[[Super League]] (3): [[Super League VI|2001]], [[Super League VIII|2003]], [[Super League X|2005]] *[[Challenge Cup]] (2): [[2000 Challenge Cup|2000]], [[2003 Challenge Cup|2003]] *[[World Club Challenge]] (2): [[2002 World Club Challenge|2002]], [[2004 World Club Challenge|2004]] * [[League Leaders' Shield]] (3): [[Super League IV|1999]], [[Super League VI|2001]], [[Super League VIII|2003]] ;Leeds * [[Super League]] (6): [[Super League XII|2007]], [[Super League XIII|2008]], [[Super Challenge]] (2): [[2008 World Club Challenge|2008]], [[2012 World Club Challenge|2012]] * [[League Leaders' Shield]] (2): [[Super League XIV|2009]], [[Super League XX|2015]] ===Individual=== *[[Man of Steel Award]]: [[Super League VIII|2003]] ===Orders and special awards=== *[[Order of the British Empire|Member of the Order of the British Empire]] (MBE): 2012 ==Other work== Since retiring from rugby, Peacock has become a [[motivational speaker]], including aspects of leadership mentoring and delivering [[keynote]] speeches for businesses. He developed a fifty-day "Building Champions" mentoring programme in 2016,{{cite web |last1=Agency (theliftagency.com) |first1=The Lift |title=Mentoring Thanks |url=http://Www.jamiepeacock.co.uk/mentoring/ |website=Jamie Peacock |access-date=20 December 2023 |language=en}} which over 1000 people have completed, as well as the "Be A Champion" wellbeing programme, which has an accompanying book.{{cite web |last1=Agency (theliftagency.com) |first1=The Lift |title=30 Day Wellbeing Programme |url=http://Www.jamiepeacock.co.uk/30-day-wellbeing-programme/ |website=Jamie Peacock |access-date=20 December 2023 |language=en}} Peacock also makes media appearances providing insight and opinion on rugby. ==Personal life== Peacock resides in Leeds, England and is the father of three children{{Cite web |last=Agency (theliftagency.com) |first=The Lift |title=Profile |url=https://www.jamiepeacock.co.uk/profile/ |access-date=2025-08-21 |website=Jamie Peacock |language=en}} from his previous marriage. He released his autobiography ''No White Flag'' in 2008, co-written with Phil Caplan.{{cn|date=November 2025}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.jamiepeacock.co.uk/ Jamie Peacock's Official Website] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20080827124103/http://leedsrugby.dnsupdate.co.uk/leedsrhinos/Squad/squad-page.asp?ID=150 Leeds Rhinos profile] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20060415102304/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2001/ashes/gb/peacock.stm 2001 Ashes profile] {{Navboxes |title= Bradford Bulls squads | bg=black | fg=#FDE910 | bordercolor=red |list1= {{Bradford Bulls -
